question_title: Will the interest in “arundel mills mall” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: A shooting at Arundel Mills Mall on March 28 caused search interest to spike to 100, which had already decayed to 34 by March 30 (start date). The incident is resolved (3 arrested, 1 non-fatal injury). Pre-spike Saturday baseline for this term is ~18, and April 11 falls on a Saturday. The expected end value of ~18 represents a ~16 point decrease from the start value of 34, far exceeding the ±3 threshold. Monte Carlo simulation (100k runs) with baseline values, residual decay, measurement noise, and small probability of new events strongly supports "Decreases" at ~94%.

factors.description: Post-spike decay: Mall shooting on Mar 28 caused spike to 100, already decayed to 34 by Mar 30. News cycle for resolved local shooting (3 arrested, 1 injury, no deaths) is 2-3 days. By Apr 11 (12 days later), interest should be at baseline.

Pre-spike Saturday baseline is ~18: Apr 11 is a Saturday. The two pre-spike Saturdays both showed value of 18. Start value of 34 minus expected ~18 = ~16 point decrease, far exceeding the ±3 threshold.

Threshold arithmetic strongly favors Decreases: 'Doesn't change' requires end value 31-37, meaning interest must stay ~2x above normal Saturday baseline for 12 more days after a resolved incident. Very unlikely.
Small probability of new event: A new newsworthy incident at the mall or follow-up legal proceedings could re-spike interest, but the case appears resolved with arrests already made.
SerpAPI measurement uncertainty: ±2 points discrepancy between pytrends and SerpAPI possible, but the expected gap (~16 points) is so large it won't change the outcome category.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (94%)**

The key dynamics:

1. **Post-spike decay**: A shooting at Arundel Mills Mall on Mar 28 spiked interest to 100. By Mar 30 (start date), it had already crashed to 34. The incident is resolved — 3 suspects arrested, 1 non-fatal injury. This type of local news event has a 2-3 day news cycle at most.

2. **Baseline reversion**: Pre-spike Saturday values were consistently ~18. April 11 is a Saturday, so the expected end value is ~18 — a decrease of ~16 points from the start value of 34, far exceeding the ±3 "Decreases" threshold.

3. **Threshold arithmetic**: "Doesn't change" (31-37) would require interest to stay ~2x above normal baseline for 12 more days after a resolved shooting — extremely unlikely. "Increases" (>37) would require a new catalyst.

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| Decreases | 94% |
| Increases | 4% |
| Doesn't change | 2% |
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “arundel mills mall” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42960)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=arundel%20mills%20mall&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=arundel%20mills%20mall&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11) for 2026-04-11 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-30. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "arundel mills mall",
  "date": "2026-03-12 2026-04-11",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
